subject: fix(a11y): SettingsPane View Level uses native fieldset + radio inputs
body:
The View Level radiogroup was a custom ARIA pattern with three
`<div role="radio" tabIndex={0}>` elements. Three accessibility issues:

  1. Every option had `tabIndex={0}` — Tab cycled through all 3
     individually instead of jumping past the radiogroup as a unit.
  2. No arrow-key navigation between options. WAI-ARIA radiogroup
     pattern requires Up/Down/Left/Right to move focus between radios;
     the original onKeyDown handled only Enter/Space.
  3. Screen readers announced "1 of 3" three times per group (once
     per div), instead of once for the whole group with each radio
     just announcing its label + checked state.

Replaced with the native HTML pattern:
  - `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` for the group container/label
  - `<input type="radio" name="view-level">` per option (HTML radio
    group association via shared `name` attribute)
  - `<label>` wrapping each input + descriptive content (clicking
    anywhere on the label toggles the input via native `for`-less
    label association)
  - DaisyUI `radio radio-primary` for the visual radio bullet
  - `aria-describedby` linking each input to its description text

Native HTML handles all three problems for free:
  - Roving tabindex: only ONE radio in a group is in the tab order
    at a time (the checked one if any, otherwise the first)
  - Arrow-key navigation: Up/Down/Left/Right move focus between
    radios in the same `name` group, automatically checking the
    focused one
  - Screen reader announces "View Level, radio group, Executive,
    1 of 3, checked" for the group + first radio, then "Management,
    2 of 3, not checked" etc. as user navigates

The visual layout (TOGGLE_ROW_CLASSES) is unchanged. The radio
bullet is `order-2` so it appears on the right of the row (matching
the previous checkmark icon position) instead of the default left.

Verified:
  - vite build clean
  - 60/60 tests pass
  - .radio + .radio-primary classes ship in built CSS

Tags: fix, a11y, ui, daisyui
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-audit
Semver: patch
